10	# ADR-007: Federation Topology (Star, Active-Spoke Hybrid)
11	
12	**Status:** Accepted
13	**Date:** 2026-05-02
14	**Context:** Each `ddx server` has stable node identity, persisted state, and a
15	project registry (FEAT-020). The web UI (FEAT-021) uses `/nodes/:nodeId/...` as
16	its canonical URL grammar and was anticipated to fan out across nodes in a
17	future "coordinator" model. Story 14 (FEAT-026) makes that future concrete:
18	multiple ddx-server nodes need to present a unified view of beads, runs, and
19	projects across machines, while the spokes remain fully usable on their own.
20	
21	## Decision
22	
23	Federation is a **star topology** with an **active-spoke hybrid** authority
24	model:
25	
26	- One node runs as the **hub** (`ddx server --hub-mode`) and exposes
27	  `/api/federation/*` plus federated read aggregations.
28	- Other nodes run as **spokes** (`ddx server --hub=<host>`) and push
29	  registration + heartbeat to the hub. A node may run as both (`hub_spoke`).
30	- Spokes remain full ddx-servers; if the hub dies, spokes keep working
31	  autonomously and their UIs remain directly reachable as a fallback path.
32	- Reads are aggregated by the hub via fan-out over the spokes' existing
33	  `/graphql` endpoints. Writes are **never broadcast** — the hub may forward a
34	  mutation to the owning spoke, but federation itself is read-only in v1.
35	
36	### Naming Convention
37	
38	Federation always uses **federation / hub / spoke**. Other terms are reserved or
39	avoided:
40	
41	| Term | Status | Reason |
42	|------|--------|--------|
43	| federation, hub, spoke, hub_spoke | Use | Adopted vocabulary for this feature |
44	| coordinator | Avoid | Already used for the per-project bead-landing serializer (FEAT-010 `coordinatorRegistry`, `land_coordinator.go`, `coordinatorMetricsByProject`); overloading the term causes confusion |
45	| primary, replica | Avoid | Implies replication semantics; federation is registration + read fan-out, not data replication |
46	| leader, follower | Avoid | Implies consensus / failover semantics not present in v1 |
47	| master, worker | Avoid | Overloaded with FEAT-006 worker pool |
48	
49	This convention applies to code (package names, identifiers, log messages),
50	APIs (endpoint paths, GraphQL field names, JSON schema), CLI flags, and
51	documentation.
52	
53	## Topology
54	
55	### Star (one hub, N spokes)
56	
57	- v1 fan-out is **O(spokes)** per federated query. Concurrency is bounded
58	  (default 8 in flight) and each spoke call has a per-request timeout (default
59	  2s for register/heartbeat, 5s for read fan-out).
60	- The hub does not relay between spokes; spokes never talk to each other.
61	- Failover is **out of scope**. There is exactly one hub. A new hub is brought
62	  up by an operator restart; spokes will re-register on their next heartbeat
63	  cycle.
64	- Spoke UIs are first-class fallbacks. `/api/node` and CLI status output expose
65	  the spoke's local URL so operators have a direct path when the hub is
66	  unreachable.
67	
68	### Authority: Hybrid (push registration/heartbeat, pull data on demand)
69	
70	**Push side (spoke → hub):**
71	- `POST /api/federation/register` on startup. Registration is **idempotent on
72	  stable `node_id`**: re-register replaces the registry entry by id; URL change
73	  updates the recorded URL; a duplicate `node_id` from a different identity
74	  (mismatched name + new URL) is rejected with a logged conflict and an
75	  operator-actionable error.
76	- `POST /api/federation/heartbeat` every **30s with random jitter** in
77	  `[0, 5s]` to avoid synchronized beats from waking the hub at the same instant.
78	- `POST /api/federation/deregister` on graceful shutdown.
79	
80	**Pull side (hub → spoke):**
81	- The hub fans out to spokes' existing `/graphql` over ts-net for federated
82	  read queries. No new spoke endpoint is added for fan-out beyond what already
83	  exists for direct UI use.
84	
85	**Liveness states:**
86	- `live` — heartbeat within 30s.
87	- `stale` — no heartbeat for ≥ 2 minutes; UI shows a stale badge but the spoke
88	  is still queried in fan-out.
89	- `offline` — fan-out request to the spoke failed (connection refused, ts-net
90	  unreachable, timeout). Distinct from `stale`: a `live` heartbeat does not
91	  guarantee the spoke is reachable from the hub at query time.
92	
93	The hub registry persists at `~/.local/share/ddx/federation-state.json` so the
94	hub survives restarts and rebuilds its view from disk on startup; spokes
95	re-confirm on their next heartbeat.
96	
97	### Discovery: Explicit `--hub=<host>` Over ts-net DNS
98	
99	- `ddx server --hub-mode` exposes `/api/federation/*` on the existing listener.
100	- `ddx server --hub=<host>` registers + heartbeats on startup against
101	  `<host>:<port>`. `<host>` is typically a ts-net hostname (e.g. `eitri`).
102	- Both flags may be combined (`--hub-mode --hub=<other>`) for `hub_spoke`.
103	- mDNS / Tailscale-tag auto-discovery is **rejected for v1**: it couples DDx to
104	  Tailscale ACL policy and adds a discovery surface we don't need yet.
105	- `.ddx.yml` static config is a **follow-up**. When introduced, the
106	  precedence rule is: **flag wins over config** (consistent with ADR-006's
107	  auth-key precedence rule).
108	
109	### ts-net Policy: Default Strict, Explicit Dangerous Opt-out
110	
111	- Default behavior: federation refuses non-loopback, non-ts-net peers. The
112	  policy is enforced at `/api/federation/*` regardless of the listener that
113	  accepted the request. Loopback is permitted to support same-host
114	  `hub_spoke` topologies and integration tests.
115	- `--federation-allow-plain-http` is the explicit opt-out. It is **only valid
116	  when `--hub-mode` is also set**. When enabled:
117	  - The hub accepts plain-HTTP federation traffic (loopback or otherwise).
118	  - The hub emits a warning log on each accepted plain-HTTP registration and
119	    on startup when the flag is in effect, naming the offending peer.
120	  - Spokes that wish to send plain-HTTP must opt in symmetrically (see
121	    ambiguity #1 below — current decision: **both ends must opt in**, so
122	    spokes carry the same flag).
123	- Rationale: making the dangerous mode noisy and explicit is safer than
124	  letting users build worse external tunnels around an over-strict default.
125	  This mirrors ADR-006's posture on transport-layer auth.
126	
127	### Write Routing Contract (Story 15 Hand-off)
128	
129	Federation is **read-only aggregation in v1**. Federated read models expose
130	per-row routing metadata so a future write path can resolve the owning node:
131	
132	| Field | Type | Purpose |
133	|-------|------|---------|
134	| `node_id` | string | Stable id of the spoke that owns this row |
135	| `project_id` | string | Project id within that node |
136	| `project_url` | string | Spoke base URL (for direct fallback) |
137	| `project_path` | string | On-disk path on the owning node |
138	| `write_capability` | enum | `local`, `forwardable`, `read_only` |
139	| `status` | enum | `live`, `stale`, `offline` of the owning node |
140	
141	When Story 15 (server-side prompt execution) lands, the hub may **forward** a
142	mutation to the owning spoke based on this metadata, but it must **never
143	broadcast** writes. ADR-007 documents the contract; Story 15's design
144	references it.
145	
146	## Version Handshake
147	
148	Spokes send a version block on `POST /api/federation/register`:
149	
150	```json
151	{
152	  "node_id": "node-7029e8d6",
153	  "node_name": "bragi",
154	  "url": "https://bragi:7743",
155	  "ddx_version": "0.42.0",
156	  "schema_version": "1",
157	  "graphql_schema_version": "2026-04-29",
158	  "capabilities": ["beads", "runs", "projects", "doc-graph"]
159	}
160	```
161	
162	The hub applies this **compatibility matrix** and either accepts, marks
163	`degraded`, or rejects:
164	
165	| Spoke vs Hub | `schema_version` | `graphql_schema_version` | `ddx_version` (semver) | Result |
166	|---|---|---|---|---|
167	| Same | equal | equal | equal | **accept** (`live`) |
168	| Spoke older patch | equal | equal | older patch only | **accept** (`live`) |
169	| Spoke newer patch | equal | equal | newer patch only | **accept** (`live`) |
170	| Spoke newer minor | equal | newer date | newer minor | **accept as `degraded`** — hub queries only the capability set both sides advertise |
171	| Spoke older minor | equal | older date | older minor | **accept as `degraded`** — hub avoids fields the spoke does not advertise |
172	| Mismatched major | equal | n/a | major mismatch | **reject** at register time, with explicit error |
173	| Mismatched `schema_version` | not equal | n/a | n/a | **reject** at register time |
174	| Missing `capabilities` | n/a | n/a | n/a | **reject** — hub cannot make safe field-level decisions |
175	
176	`degraded` is surfaced in the UI as a yellow badge on the affected spoke and
177	on every row sourced from it. Reject decisions are made at register time so
178	operators see the failure immediately rather than at query time.
179	
180	## Spec Surface (Compatibility Hooks)
181	
182	ADR-007 is implemented across three feature specs:
183	
184	- **FEAT-026** (new) — owns federation end-to-end: registry schema,
185	  hub/spoke endpoints, fan-out client, federated GraphQL fields, UI routes.
186	- **FEAT-020 amendment** — adds `federation-state.json` schema, the
187	  `--hub-mode`, `--hub=<host>`, `--federation-allow-plain-http` flags, and
188	  the `node.federation_role` field on `/api/node`. Compatibility hooks only,
189	  no implementation.
190	- **FEAT-021 amendment** — adds the `/federation` overview route, hub-resolved
191	  `/nodes/:nodeId/...` routing across registered spokes, and `?scope=federation`
192	  on combined views. Compatibility hooks only, no implementation.
193	
194	## Consequences
195	
196	- **Operator clarity:** one hub, N spokes; explicit flags; explicit failure
197	  modes (`stale`, `offline`, `degraded`, conflict-rejected).
198	- **No replication, no consensus:** the hub holds an in-memory + persisted
199	  view of where data lives, not a copy of the data. Loss of the hub state file
200	  is recoverable on the next heartbeat cycle.
201	- **Spoke autonomy:** spokes do not depend on the hub for correctness. Each
202	  spoke UI remains usable; CLI and direct browser access work without the hub.
203	- **Hub fan-out cost:** O(spokes) per federated read. Bounded concurrency and
204	  per-call timeouts cap the cost; a single slow spoke cannot block the view.
205	- **Plain-HTTP escape valve is documented and noisy:** the dangerous mode is
206	  explicit, log-loud, and gated behind both `--hub-mode` and a separate flag.
207	- **Future write path is preserved:** routing metadata fields are part of
208	  every federated row, so Story 15 can layer write forwarding without a v2
209	  schema migration.
210	- **Naming discipline:** `coordinator`, `primary`, `replica`, `leader` are
211	  reserved/avoided so the federation vocabulary does not collide with
212	  pre-existing per-project subsystems.
213	
214	## Alternatives Considered
215	
216	| Alternative | Verdict | Reason |
217	|---|---|---|
218	| Star topology, push-only (spokes push all data) | Rejected | Doubles storage and forces continuous replication; federation use cases are read-aggregation, not redundancy |
219	| Star topology, pull-only (hub polls spokes) | Rejected | Hub does not know about spokes until they push; auto-discovery of spokes is explicitly out of scope |
220	| Mesh topology (every node peers with every other node) | Rejected | O(N²) connections, no clear authority, harder to reason about for v1 |
221	| Single shared SQLite over a network drive | Rejected | Lock contention, no isolation, fragile across machines |
222	| Failover / hot standby hub | Deferred | Operator restart is acceptable for v1; complexity not justified yet |
223	| mDNS / Tailscale-tag spoke discovery | Deferred | Couples DDx to Tailscale ACL policy; explicit `--hub=` is sufficient |
224	
225	## Flagged Ambiguities (resolved here unless noted)
226	
227	1. **Plain-HTTP opt-out scope.** Decision: **both ends must opt in**. A spoke
228	   that wishes to send plain-HTTP carries `--federation-allow-plain-http`; the
229	   hub must also carry it to accept. This keeps the dangerous surface
230	   symmetric and audit-loggable.
231	2. **Version-skew granularity.** Decision: matrix above. Reject on major or
232	   `schema_version` mismatch; mark `degraded` on minor/graphql-schema drift.
233	3. **`scope: LOCAL | FEDERATION` GraphQL migration.** **Open.** v1 ships
234	   parallel `federated*` fields per FEAT-026 design. A future migration to
235	   unify under a `scope` argument is allowed but not committed to a release
236	   in ADR-007.
237	4. **Heartbeat cadence config surface.** Deferred to v1.1: flag, env var, or
238	   `.ddx.yml`. v1 is hard-coded at 30s + jitter.
239	5. **Duplicate `node_id` recovery UX.** v1 hard-rejects with a logged
240	   conflict and an operator-actionable error. Soft-rebind on operator action
241	   is a follow-up.
242

11	# Feature: Federation / Hub-Spoke Coordinator
12	
13	**ID:** FEAT-026
14	**Status:** Frame
15	**Priority:** P2
16	**Owner:** DDx Team
17	
18	## Overview
19	
20	Federation lets multiple `ddx server` nodes present a unified view of beads,
21	runs, and projects across machines while each node remains fully usable on
22	its own. One node runs as the **hub**; the others run as **spokes** that
23	register and heartbeat to the hub. The hub aggregates reads by fanning out to
24	spokes' existing GraphQL APIs over ts-net; writes are never broadcast and may
25	be forwarded to the owning spoke in a future iteration (Story 15).
26	
27	ADR-007 captures the topology, authority model, ts-net policy, plain-HTTP
28	opt-out, and write-routing contract. ADR-006 supplies the transport-layer
29	authentication. FEAT-020 supplies node identity, the project registry, and
30	the persisted state model that federation-state.json extends. FEAT-021
31	supplies the `/nodes/:nodeId/...` URL grammar that the hub resolves across
32	registered spokes.
33	
34	## Problem Statement
35	
36	**Current situation:** Every `ddx server` is a single node. The UI sees only
37	the projects on its own machine; CLI tools cannot ask one server about work
38	happening on another. Operators with more than one machine — workstation +
39	server, or several developer boxes on a tailnet — must visit each UI
40	separately and stitch the picture together by hand.
41	
42	**Pain points:**
43	- No single dashboard answers "what is happening across my machines?"
44	- Combined views (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`, `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`) are
45	  pre-wired in FEAT-021 for cross-project but stop at the node boundary.
46	- Run history, bead queues, and project lists must be reconciled by hand
47	  when work spans nodes.
48	- Story 15 (server-side prompt execution) needs a routing contract for
49	  "which node owns this row" before it can forward writes safely.
50	
51	**Desired outcome:** An operator can run `ddx server --hub-mode` on one node
52	and `ddx server --hub=<host>` on the others. The hub UI surfaces a
53	`/federation` overview, all `/nodes/:nodeId/...` routes resolve across the
54	federation, and combined views accept `?scope=federation` to fan out across
55	spokes. Spoke UIs continue to work directly as a fallback.
56	
57	## Architecture
58	
59	See **ADR-007** for the topology, authority, ts-net policy, write-routing
60	contract, naming convention, and version-handshake compatibility matrix.
61	This spec describes the user-visible surface that ADR-007 makes real.
62	
63	### Roles
64	
65	| Role | Flag | Behavior |
66	|------|------|----------|
67	| `standalone` | (default) | Single node; no `/api/federation/*`; no register/heartbeat. |
68	| `hub` | `--hub-mode` | Exposes `/api/federation/*`; persists registry; fans out reads. |
69	| `spoke` | `--hub=<host>` | Registers and heartbeats to `<host>`; serves federated reads via existing `/graphql`. |
70	| `hub_spoke` | `--hub-mode --hub=<other>` | Both behaviors on the same node. |
71	
72	`/api/node` exposes the active role as `node.federation_role`
73	(see FEAT-020 amendment).
74	
75	### Federation Registry
76	
77	Persisted on the hub at `~/.local/share/ddx/federation-state.json`:
78	
79	```json
80	{
81	  "schema_version": "1",
82	  "hub": {
83	    "node_id": "node-7029e8d6",
84	    "node_name": "eitri",
85	    "started_at": "2026-05-02T19:58:33Z"
86	  },
87	  "spokes": [
88	    {
89	      "node_id": "node-bf91c204",
90	      "node_name": "bragi",
91	      "url": "https://bragi:7743",
92	      "ddx_version": "0.42.0",
93	      "schema_version": "1",
94	      "graphql_schema_version": "2026-04-29",
95	      "capabilities": ["beads", "runs", "projects", "doc-graph"],
96	      "registered_at": "2026-05-02T19:58:34Z",
97	      "last_heartbeat": "2026-05-02T20:00:01Z",
98	      "compat_status": "live",
99	      "liveness": "live"
100	    }
101	  ]
102	}
103	```
104	
105	`compat_status` is one of `live`, `degraded`, `rejected`. `liveness` is one
106	of `live`, `stale`, `offline` (see ADR-007).
107	
108	### Hub HTTP API
109	
110	| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
111	|----------|--------|---------|
112	| `/api/federation/register` | POST | Idempotent register on stable `node_id`; returns assigned compat_status |
113	| `/api/federation/heartbeat` | POST | 30s + jitter; updates `last_heartbeat` and re-asserts URL |
114	| `/api/federation/deregister` | POST | Graceful shutdown; removes spoke from registry |
115	| `/api/federation/nodes` | GET | List of `{hub, spokes[]}` with liveness + compat_status |
116	
117	All federation endpoints enforce ts-net or loopback peers unless the hub was
118	started with `--federation-allow-plain-http` (see ADR-007).
119	
120	### Federated GraphQL Read Surface
121	
122	The hub exposes parallel federated fields alongside existing local queries.
123	Existing queries remain node-local and unchanged; federated fields fan out:
124	
125	```graphql
126	type Query {
127	  federationNodes: [FederationNode!]!
128	  federatedBeads(scope: FederationScope, projectID: ID): BeadConnection!
129	  federatedRuns(scope: FederationScope, projectID: ID, layer: RunLayer): RunConnection!
130	  federatedProjects(scope: FederationScope): [Project!]!
131	}
132	
133	enum FederationScope { LOCAL FEDERATION }
134	```
135	
136	Every federated row carries the routing-metadata fields described in
137	ADR-007 (`node_id`, `project_id`, `project_url`, `project_path`,
138	`write_capability`, `status`).
139	
140	### CLI Flags
141	
142	```
143	--hub-mode                       Run as federation hub
144	--hub=<host[:port]>              Run as spoke; register with this hub
145	--federation-allow-plain-http    Allow non-ts-net peers (hub-mode required to accept;
146	                                 spokes need it too to send)
147	```
148	
149	Precedence (matches ADR-006): flag > env > config (`.ddx.yml` config is a
150	follow-up).
151	
152	### UI Routes (delivered by FEAT-021 amendment)
153	
154	- `/federation` — federation overview (hub + spokes, liveness, compat).
155	- `/nodes/:nodeId/...` — when the node is a registered spoke, the hub
156	  resolves and serves the route from the spoke's data.
157	- Combined views (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`, `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`) accept
158	  `?scope=federation` to fan out across all spokes.
159	
160	## Requirements
161	
162	### Functional
163	
164	1. A node started with `--hub-mode` exposes `/api/federation/*` and persists
165	   the federation registry across restarts.
166	2. A node started with `--hub=<host>` registers with that hub on startup
167	   (idempotent on `node_id`), heartbeats every 30s ± 5s jitter, and
168	   deregisters on graceful shutdown.
169	3. Re-registration with the same `node_id` updates the recorded URL and
170	   `last_heartbeat`. A duplicate `node_id` from a mismatched identity is
171	   rejected with a logged conflict.
172	4. Hub restart preserves the registry from disk and the next heartbeat cycle
173	   re-confirms each spoke without operator intervention.
174	5. Heartbeats overdue by more than 2 minutes mark the spoke `stale`. Fan-out
175	   failures mark it `offline`. The two states are distinct in the registry
176	   and in the UI.
177	6. Version handshake at register time follows the compatibility matrix in
178	   ADR-007: incompatible spokes are rejected, compatible-but-newer/older
179	   minors are marked `degraded`.
180	7. The hub's federated read fan-out is bounded by per-call timeout (default
181	   5s) and max concurrency (default 8). Partial results are returned with
182	   per-node error annotations rather than failing the whole query.
183	8. Default federation transport is ts-net or loopback only; non-ts-net peers
184	   are accepted only when both ends carry `--federation-allow-plain-http`,
185	   and the hub emits a warning log on each accepted plain-HTTP exchange.
186	9. Federated rows expose routing metadata (`node_id`, `project_id`,
187	   `project_url`, `project_path`, `write_capability`, `status`) so Story 15
188	   can later forward writes to the owning node.
189	10. The hub UI exposes `/federation` and resolves `/nodes/:nodeId/...` for
190	    registered spokes; spoke UIs remain directly reachable as a fallback.
191	
192	### Non-Functional
193	
194	- Federation registration adds < 50ms to spoke startup in the happy path.
195	- Heartbeat overhead is bounded: one POST per spoke per 30s ± jitter.
196	- Federated fan-out for 5 spokes returns within 1.5× the slowest spoke (or
197	  the timeout, whichever is smaller).
198	- A single offline spoke does not block fan-out for other spokes.
199	- The federation-state.json file is written with mode `0600`; the
200	  `~/.local/share/ddx/` directory is `0700` (matches FEAT-020).
201	
202	## User Stories
203	
204	### US-095: Operator Promotes One Node to Hub
205	**As an** operator with several DDx machines on a tailnet
206	**I want** to designate one of them as the federation hub
207	**So that** I can see all my machines' work in a single dashboard
208	
209	**Acceptance Criteria:**
210	- Given I start `ddx server --hub-mode`, when I call `GET /api/federation/nodes`,
211	  then I see the hub itself and an empty spoke list
212	- Given another node has not yet started as a spoke, then it is invisible
213	  to the hub
214	- Given I open the hub UI, then `/federation` is reachable and shows the hub
215	  with no spokes
216	
217	### US-096: Operator Joins a Spoke to the Federation
218	**As an** operator running DDx on a second machine
219	**I want** to point that node at the hub
220	**So that** the hub can show its beads, runs, and projects
221	
222	**Acceptance Criteria:**
223	- Given the hub is running and I start `ddx server --hub=<hub-host>`, then
224	  the spoke registers within 1s and appears in `/api/federation/nodes`
225	- Given I restart the spoke, then it re-registers idempotently — the
226	  registry entry updates `last_heartbeat` and `url` but does not duplicate
227	- Given the spoke version is incompatible per the ADR-007 matrix, then
228	  registration fails fast with a clear error and the spoke logs the reason
229	
230	### US-097: Operator Sees Federated Work in the Hub UI
231	**As an** operator using the hub UI
232	**I want** combined views to fan out across spokes
233	**So that** my dashboard reflects everything happening on my machines
234	
235	**Acceptance Criteria:**
236	- Given two spokes are registered, when I open `/nodes/:hubId/beads?scope=federation`,
237	  then I see beads from the hub and both spokes with node + project badges
238	- Given one spoke is offline, then its rows are absent and the UI shows an
239	  `offline` badge for that node rather than failing the page
240	- Given a spoke is `stale` (no heartbeat in 2m), then its rows still appear
241	  with a `stale` badge
242	- Given I click a row originating from a spoke, then I navigate to
243	  `/nodes/:spokeId/...` resolved through the hub
244	
245	### US-098: Operator Falls Back to a Spoke UI Directly
246	**As an** operator when the hub is unreachable
247	**I want** to open the spoke UI directly
248	**So that** I can keep working without waiting for the hub to recover
249	
250	**Acceptance Criteria:**
251	- Given the hub is down, when I open the spoke's local URL, then its UI
252	  works exactly as in the standalone case
253	- Given the hub returns later, then the spoke re-registers on the next
254	  heartbeat without operator intervention
255	
256	### US-099: Operator Sees Version Skew Surfaced in the UI
257	**As an** operator running mixed DDx versions
258	**I want** to know when a spoke is degraded due to schema or version skew
259	**So that** I can plan upgrades without surprises
260	
261	**Acceptance Criteria:**
262	- Given a spoke registered as `degraded`, then `/federation` shows it with
263	  a yellow badge and a tooltip explaining the skew
264	- Given any federated row sourced from that spoke, then the row carries the
265	  same `degraded` badge
266	
267	### US-100: Operator Confirms Default Transport Is ts-net Only
268	**As a** security-conscious operator
269	**I want** federation to refuse non-ts-net peers by default
270	**So that** a misconfigured network does not leak federation traffic
271	
272	**Acceptance Criteria:**
273	- Given the hub started without `--federation-allow-plain-http`, when a
274	  non-ts-net non-loopback peer attempts to register, then the request is
275	  refused with a clear error and a log line
276	- Given the hub started with `--federation-allow-plain-http`, then the same
277	  request is accepted, the hub logs a warning, and `/federation` shows a
278	  policy banner
279	
280	## Out of Scope
281	
282	- Federation write fan-out / broadcast (writes are read-only-aggregated in v1).
283	- Hub failover / hot standby.
284	- Auto-discovery of spokes (mDNS, Tailscale tag-based discovery).
285	- `.ddx.yml` static configuration of `--hub` / `--hub-mode` (follow-up).
286	- Heartbeat cadence configuration surface (hard-coded 30s ± 5s in v1).
287	- Soft-rebind UX for duplicate `node_id` conflicts (v1 hard-rejects).
288	- Mesh / peer-to-peer topology.
289	- Cross-spoke direct calls (spokes never talk to each other).
290	
291	## Dependencies
292	
293	- ADR-006 (ts-net authentication)
294	- ADR-007 (federation topology — this feature's design contract)
295	- FEAT-020 (server node state and project registry — extended with
296	  `federation_role` and federation flags)
297	- FEAT-021 (multi-node dashboard UI — extended with `/federation` route and
298	  `?scope=federation`)
299	- FEAT-002 (server HTTP API)
300	- FEAT-004, FEAT-006, FEAT-010 (data sources fanned out by the hub)
301	
302	## References
303	
304	- `/tmp/story-14-final.md` — story plan and bead breakdown
305	- ADR-007 — federation topology decision and compatibility matrix
306	- FEAT-020 amendment — `federation-state.json` schema, federation flags,
307	  `node.federation_role`
308	- FEAT-021 amendment — `/federation` overview, hub-resolved routes,
309	  `?scope=federation` on combined views

# ddx-c739bd73 execution notes

## State at attempt start

The bead's deliverables already existed at HEAD (`5c8f6f7f`):

- `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-007-federation-topology.md` — authored
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md` — authored
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-020-server-node-state.md` — federation amendment present (lines 156–211, 315–318)
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md` — federation amendment present (lines 58, 75–76, 562–592)

These all landed in commit `6e386920` ("docs(helix): ADR-007 federation
topology + FEAT-026 frame + FEAT-020/021 amendments [ddx-1d4bfbf3]") on
`main` before this bead claimed.

## What this attempt changed

`ddx doc audit` reported a `cycle: ADR-007 -> FEAT-026 -> ADR-007`. ADR-007
listed `FEAT-026` in its `depends_on` while FEAT-026 also depends on ADR-007.
ADRs document decisions and should not depend on the features that consume
them. Removed `FEAT-026` from `ADR-007.depends_on`. Federation cycle gone.

## Audit residue (out of scope for this bead)

After the fix, `ddx doc audit` still reports:

- `duplicate_id (84)` — every duplicate's `relatedPath` is under
  `.agents/skills/docs/...`, a mirror of the `docs/` tree placed there by
  the agent-skills install path. Pre-existing; unrelated to federation.
- `cycle (1)` — `FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-020
  -> SD-013 -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002`. None of these arcs were touched by
  federation work; pre-existing across multiple feature specs.
- `missing_dep (1)` — `helix.workflow.principles` references `helix.workflow`
  which is not in the graph. Pre-existing.

AC4 ("ddx doc audit clean") is satisfied for the federation slice this bead
owns. The residual issues belong to other features and the skills-tree
install duplication, and fixing them would touch files outside this bead's
scope.
